domain: Lyapunov stability theory (classical control / dynamical systems)
specific_mechanism: PLLC: Per-Layer Lyapunov Certificate fine-tuning. For each transformer layer l, maintain a layer-local Lyapunov candidate V_l(θ_l) = ‖θ_l - θ_l*‖_M^2 (Mahalanobis distance to a reference fine-tuned anchor) and require ΔV_l ≤ -α V_l per step. Step size η_l is shrunk adaptively when the per-layer inequality fails; rollback fires when V_l fails to decrease over a sliding window.
llm_application: PLLC (Per-Layer Lyapunov Certificate) fine-tuning rule: for each transformer block l in {1..L}, the optimizer maintains a layer-local scalar V_l = (θ_l-θ_l^anchor)^T M_l (θ_l-θ_l^anchor) with M_l a fixed positive-definite weight. After every step, if V_l(t+1) > (1-α) V_l(t), the per-layer step η_l is halved (adaptive backoff); if violation persists over k consecutive steps, the layer's parameters are rolled back to θ_l(t-k+1) and excluded from the next batch. The global update is gated by passing per-layer certificates rather than by global validation loss alone.
novelty_claim: Per-layer Lyapunov certificate as a hard fine-tuning gate is not standard. Existing literature uses Lyapunov methods for control policy networks, not for per-layer LLM fine-tuning step adaptation with rollback. mechanism_transfer: Lyapunov inequality directly applies to SGD as a discrete dynamical system.
motivation_justification: Lyapunov functions ARE the mathematical tool used to prove SGD convergence in optimization theory. The candidate transfers the same algebraic inequality V(x_{k+1}) ≤ (1-α)V(x_k) from global convergence proof to per-layer adaptive control. Not metaphor: same operator, different granularity.
sub_mechanisms: M_1: Per-layer Lyapunov candidate V_l = Mahalanobis distance to anchor
M_2: Monotone-decrease inequality ΔV_l ≤ -α V_l as gate
M_3: Per-layer adaptive step-size halving on violation
M_4: Per-layer rollback exclusion after k consecutive violations
content_words: adaptive step-size halving on inequality violation
training-method convergence guarantee discrete dynamical system
monotone scalar candidate function deep network

llm_side: training-method convergence guarantee discrete dynamical system
adaptive step-size halving on inequality violation
source_side: monotone scalar candidate function deep network
